summary: Cleaned‑up soundboard recording of an early‑Dead show with a beloved Alice D. Millionaire performance and generally good band energy despite some high‑end loss.
review: The three‑set Matrix concert from December 1, 1966 captures the band in solid form, highlighted by a fan‑favorite rendition of “Alice D. Millionaire.” The soundboard source has had the original hiss removed, giving a clearer listen, though some listeners note a slight loss of high‑frequency sparkle and occasional song‑order confusion.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and appreciate the cleaned‑up recording; minor technical shortcomings keep the rating just below top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Original tape hiss eliminated in newer source, but high‑end frequencies are a bit recessed; some reviewers note songs are mixed out of order (Lindy, Dancin' in the Street, Me & My Uncle).

show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set early‑Dead set at the Matrix with strong audience interaction and solid playing; setlist is well‑documented though track order confusion is mentioned.
